name: Grade 1 Living Library — Semester 2
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

lessons.title: Practice research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

resources.title: AASL National School Library Standards
resources.description: Planning reference for this Grade 1 Living Library pathway.

units.title: Investigate: research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to research with books, diverse perspectives, source choices, and sharing discoveries.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
